leadText: A Mega Ring (Japanese: メガリング Mega Ring) is a Key Item introduced in Generation VI. It is a Key Stone accessory that enables Mega Evolution. Its counterpart in Pokémon Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire is the Mega Bracelet.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Effect [ edit | edit source ] Allows the player to Mega Evolve their Pokémon if they hold the appropriate Mega Stone . Each Trainer can only Mega Evolve one Pokémon per battle.

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description X Y This ring contains an untold power that somehow enables Pokémon carrying a Mega Stone to Mega Evolve in battle. OR AS S M US UM P E Sw Sh BD SP LA This ring contains an untold power that somehow enables Pokémon carrying Mega Stones to Mega Evolve in battle. S V ZA This ring contains an unknown power that somehow enables a Pokémon carrying a Mega Stone to Mega Evolve in battle.

sections.title: In the core series games
sections.text: Contents 1 In the core series games 1.1 History 1.2 Pokémon X and Y 1.3 Pokémon Legends: Z-A 1.4 Non-player character appearances 1.5 Price 1.6 Effect 1.7 Description 1.8 Acquisition 1.9 Appearance 1.9.1 Artwork In the core series games [ edit | edit source ] History [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon X and Y [ edit | edit source ] At the Tower of Mastery , Gurkinn offers a single Mega Ring to the player and their friends, but due to only having a single Mega Ring to give away, requests that they decide among themselves who should receive it. Of the group, only the player and Serena / Calem are interested in receiving it, so the two battle to decide who should receive it. Upon defeating Serena/Calem, the player must obtain the Rumble Badge at the Shalour Gym and then go to the top of the Tower of Mastery to receive the ring from Korrina . After the player has defeated their rival in Kiloude City once, Professor Sycamore will upgrade the player's Mega Ring at the Anistar Sundial by having the player touch the sundial. Once upgraded, the player can then find hidden Mega Stones around Kalos from 8:00 PM to 9:00 PM. Pokémon Legends: Z-A [ edit | edit source ] Mega Ring in Pokémon Legends: Z-A AZ is rumored to have traveled to Hoenn , where he researched the origins of Mega Evolution to satisfy his own curiosity, then taught Quasartico Inc. how to make Mega Rings with his research. When Ange awakened within Prism Tower , it started unleading Mega Power , causing wild Pokémon to undergo Rogue Mega Evolution . To stop the tower, Quasartico planned to find the strongest Mega Evolution user by providing certain Trainers of the Z-A Royale from Rank G and above with Mega Rings in order for them to use Mega Evolution. Non-player character appearances [ edit | edit source ] In Pokémon X and Y , after the player has visited Kiloude City and participated in a battle at the Battle Maison , Calem/Serena will battle the player once per day at the northern part of the city, having obtained their own Mega Ring. In Pokémon Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire , Courtney OR / Matt AS use Mega Rings during the Delta Episode , although they are not seen on their artwork. In Pokémon Sun, Moon , Ultra Sun, and Ultra Moon , Sina and Dexio use white Mega Rings, which they each wear around their left wrist, though only Dexio has the opportunity to use it outside the Battle Tree . In Pokémon Legends: Z-A , Urbain / Taunie , Naveen , and Lida each have a Mega Ring. Each Trainer can only Mega Evolve one Pokémon per battle. sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Trivia [ edit | edit source ] Lysandre 's Mega Ring Lysandre 's Key Stone accessory is also known as a Mega Ring; however, it is a finger ring, not a wrist ring. Grisham and Griselle also wear similar rings embedded with their Key Stones, seemingly in imitation of Lysandre.
